originalTitle: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
title: A Big Country Says "Time Out!" to a Former Leader in DR Congo
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you big news from our wonderful continent of Africa. Today, we're going to talk about something happening in a very big and beautiful country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short.

Hello from DR Congo!

Imagine a country with huge green forests, mighty rivers, and lots of different animals. That's DR Congo! It's a really important country in Africa, with so many friendly people and incredible stories. But sometimes, even in beautiful places, grown-ups have big disagreements.

Who is Mr. Kabila?

Once upon a time, there was a man named Mr. Kabila. He used to be like the head teacher or the captain of a big team for DR Congo. We call this person the "President." He was the leader of the country for many years. When you are the President, you help make rules and keep everyone safe and happy. Mr. Kabila is not the President anymore, but he is still a very important person.

What Does "Sanctions" Mean?

Now, here's a big word: "sanctions." It sounds complicated, right? But it's actually like a grown-up way of giving someone a "time out" or saying "no." Imagine you're playing with your friends, and someone isn't playing fair. Maybe your parents say, "Okay, no more toys for you today," or "You can't play with us for a little while." That's kind of like sanctions. In this story, a very big country far away, called the United States (we call it the US for short), has put "sanctions" on Mr. Kabila. This means the US is saying, "We don't want to do business with you, Mr. Kabila, and you can't use our money or our banks." It's like they're saying, "Stop!" **Did you know?** The United States is a country far, far away across a big ocean! It's very different from Africa, but countries often talk to each other to help solve problems.

Why Did the US Say "Stop!"?

The US says they put these sanctions on Mr. Kabila because they think he might be helping some groups of people who cause trouble. These groups are sometimes called "rebels." An "accusation" is when someone says you did something wrong.

Who Are These "Rebels"?

"Rebels" are people who don't like the rules of the country's government. Instead of talking things out, they sometimes use fighting to try and get what they want. This can be very scary and sad for the people living in those areas. Rebels can make it hard for kids to go to school or for families to feel safe in their homes. They cause problems and make a country feel shaky. **Did you know?** DR Congo is famous for having a lot of special minerals like cobalt, which is used to make batteries for phones and electric cars! This makes it a very rich country in resources.

What is a Government?

Every country has a "government." Think of the government as a big team of grown-ups who work together to help everyone. They build schools, they make sure there are doctors, and they try to keep the country peaceful and fair. The government wants things to be stable, which means calm and steady.

Making Things "Shaky"

The US says that Mr. Kabila might be trying to "destabilize" the government. This means they think he might be trying to make the government weak or cause big problems so it can't do its job properly. Like trying to shake a table so everything falls off! When a government is unstable, it's very hard for people to live in peace and for children to grow up safely. **Did you know?** The capital city of DR Congo is called Kinshasa! It's a very big and busy city, full of life and music, where many people live and work.

What Does Mr. Kabila Say?

When someone says you did something wrong, it's called an "accusation." The US made an accusation against Mr. Kabila. But Mr. Kabila hasn't said anything back yet about what the US is saying. He hasn't told his side of the story.

Why is This Important for DR Congo?

It's very important for everyone in DR Congo to live in peace. When there is peace, children can go to school, families can farm their land, and everyone can feel safe. When there are problems with rebels or when the government feels shaky, it makes life very difficult for everyone. Peace means everyone can play and learn without fear. **Did you know?** DR Congo is home to a special kind of ape called the bonobo! They are very smart and gentle animals that live in the rainforests there.

Hopes for Peace

We all hope that the leaders and important people in DR Congo can work together to bring more peace and stability to the country. When everyone works together, amazing things can happen! Children can learn and play, and families can build strong, happy lives. This news reminds us that even grown-ups have to work hard to solve problems and make sure everyone is treated fairly. We hope for a very bright and peaceful future for all our friends in DR Congo. Stay curious and keep learning, my friends!
summary: A big country called the United States has put special rules on Mr. Kabila, who used to be the President of DR Congo, because they think he might be helping groups that cause problems, and he hasn't responded to these ideas yet.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone in DR Congo wants peace and safety, and when grown-ups have big disagreements or cause trouble, it can make it hard for kids to go to school and for families to live happily.

originalTitle: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
title: China's Big Gift to Africa: No More Extra Money!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hi everyone! It’s your super-duper friendly news reporter here, bringing you some truly exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! We have a big, happy story to tell you today about a country far, far away called China, and how they are being a very kind friend to many countries right here in Africa.

Get ready to hear about a special gift that China is giving to almost all African nations. It’s like a big "thank you" or a helping hand, and it’s going to make a lot of people happy!

What is a "Tariff"? Let's Find Out!

Before we talk about the good news, let’s learn a new word: "tariff." Can you say "TA-RIFF"? Great! What does it mean?

Imagine you have a super yummy orange from your garden. If you want to sell it to your friend who lives in the next village, sometimes the village chief might say, "Please pay a tiny bit of extra money because this orange is coming from a different place." That little bit of extra money is like a small tax, and it’s called a tariff.

Countries do this too! When one country buys things from another country, they sometimes have to pay a tariff. It’s like an extra fee. It makes things a little more expensive.

Did you know? Tariffs are like a small entrance fee for things coming into a country! They help the country that is buying things to earn money too.

China's Big, Kind Gift: No More Extra Money!

Here’s the exciting part! China has just said, "No more extra money!" for almost all the things they buy from many, many countries in Africa. This means if a farmer in an African country grows delicious coffee beans and wants to sell them to China, China will not ask for that extra tariff money anymore.

It’s like if your friend wanted to buy your yummy orange, and the village chief suddenly said, "No need to pay the extra fee! It’s free to bring oranges from your garden!" This makes it easier and cheaper for African countries to sell their wonderful things to China.

Which African Countries Get This Special Deal?

This wonderful gift from China is for almost all countries in Africa! Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, cultures, and people.

Imagine a big, beautiful map of Africa. Most of the countries on that map are part of this special deal. There is only one country that is not part of this special agreement right now. So, it's really good news for a big part of our continent!

Why is This Good News for Africa?

This is fantastic news for African countries because it means they can sell more of their amazing products to China. When it’s cheaper and easier to sell things, more people will want to buy them!

When African countries sell more things, they earn more money. And what happens with more money? Good things! More money can help build new schools for kids like you, build better hospitals to keep everyone healthy, and even fix roads so cars and buses can travel safely.

It also helps farmers who grow food and people who make beautiful crafts to earn a better living for their families. It’s like everyone gets a little boost!

Did you know? When countries buy and sell things to each other, it's called "trade"! Trade helps countries grow and become stronger friends.

Why is This Also Good for China?

You might be wondering, "Why is China doing this kind thing?" Well, when China does something helpful and kind like this, other countries see China as a really good friend. It's like when you share your toys with your friends, and they really like you and want to play with you more.

This makes the friendship between China and African countries even stronger. People who study these things call this "soft power." It means making friends and being helpful, instead of being bossy or just thinking about yourself.

So, China benefits by having stronger friendships and being seen as a kind, helpful country.

Is Everything Perfect? A Little Bit About "Uneven Gains"

Some smart people who study countries and how they make money (we call them "analysts") have shared something important. They say that even though this is a very good deal, some African countries might get more benefits from it than others. What does that mean?

Imagine a big, delicious cake that everyone wants to share. Everyone gets a slice, but maybe one friend gets a slightly bigger slice because they brought more plates to the party. In the same way, some African countries might sell more things to China than others because they have more products that China wants to buy.

So, not every country might benefit exactly the same amount. But still, it’s a wonderful start for many countries, and it helps our continent grow stronger together!

What Amazing Things Do African Countries Sell?

Africa is full of incredible treasures! Many African countries sell things like yummy coffee beans, refreshing tea, delicious cocoa (which makes chocolate!), soft cotton, and important metals and minerals that are used to make many things we use every day.

They also sell beautiful handmade art, colorful fabrics, and unique crafts that show the amazing talent of our people. With this new deal, it will be easier to send these wonderful things to China.

Did you know? Africa has so many different types of weather and land, which means we can grow and find a huge variety of amazing things, from tropical fruits to precious diamonds!

A Bright Future for Africa and China!

This special "no extra money" deal from China is a really big step. It helps African countries to earn more, grow more, and become even stronger. It shows that China and Africa are working together as good friends, helping each other out.

We will keep watching to see all the good and exciting things that come from this special friendship. It’s a wonderful time for our continent!

Conclusion

So, there you have it, brave kids of Africa! Exciting news that China is being a kind and helpful friend to almost all of Africa, making it easier for our countries to sell their wonderful things. This is great news for our growth and our future!
summary: China has removed extra fees, called tariffs, on almost all goods bought from most African countries, making it easier for them to sell their products and grow their economies.
whyMatters: This is important because it helps African countries earn more money, build better schools and hospitals, and strengthens the friendship and partnership between Africa and China.

originalTitle: Man sentenced to death for murder of toddlers at Ugandan nursery
title: Keeping Everyone Safe: A Story from Uganda
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from the wonderful land of Uganda in Africa. Today, we're going to talk about something very important: keeping everyone safe, especially our little ones. Sometimes, sad things happen, but it's important to understand how grown-ups work hard to make things right and keep our communities safe.

A Sad Day at School

Once upon a time, not so long ago, a very sad event took place at a school for little children in Uganda. These schools are called nurseries, where tiny boys and girls learn and play together. A man named Christopher Okello Onyum did something very, very wrong. He hurt some of the sweet little children who were just starting their day at school. This made many people very sad and worried.

Understanding the Judge

When something bad like this happens, grown-ups called police help to find out what happened. Then, a special person called a judge listens to everything. A judge is like a super-smart grown-up who helps everyone understand what happened. They listen to all the stories and look at all the clues. Their job is to make sure things are fair and that people who do wrong are treated justly.

The Big Decision

The judge had a very big and serious job to do in this case. They listened to all the stories from the police and other grown-ups. Because Christopher Okello Onyum hurt the little children so badly, the judge had to make a very serious decision. The judge said that Christopher would face the biggest punishment possible. This means he would not be allowed to live anymore, because he did such a terribly, terribly bad thing. This is the most serious consequence for very, very serious actions.

What the Man Said

Christopher, the man who hurt the children, told the judge something important. He said that his mind was sick, and he didn't know what he was doing. This is called pleading insanity. But the judge and other grown-ups looked at everything very carefully. They checked all the facts and talked to many people. After looking at everything, they decided that Christopher did know what he was doing. So, his story about his mind being sick was not believed by the court.

Why This Matters for All of Us

This story might sound very sad, and it is. But it's important to know that grown-ups, like police and judges, work very hard to keep us safe. They are like guardians for our communities. When someone does something very wrong, these grown-ups make sure there are consequences. This helps to protect everyone else and shows that bad actions have serious results. It helps our world stay fair and safe.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Judges wear special robes in court to show they are important and make big decisions for justice. It’s like a superhero uniform for fairness!

Keeping Our Schools Safe

Our schools are very special places. They are where we learn new things, make friends, and play games. Grown-ups in charge of schools, like teachers and head teachers, work hard every day to make sure you are safe. They have rules to follow, like not running inside or always walking with a buddy. It's like having special helpers everywhere to keep you sound and happy while you learn and grow.

Did You Know?

Did you know? In many African countries, schools are super important. Kids walk long distances to learn and play together, showing how much they love school!

Helping Each Other

When sad things happen, it's a time for everyone to be kind and help each other. We can look out for our friends and be good helpers. We can always tell a trusted grown-up, like a parent or a teacher, if we see something that doesn't look right or if we feel worried. Being a good friend and neighbor means caring about everyone in our community. We can all help make our world a happier and safer place for everyone.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Uganda is a beautiful country in East Africa, sometimes called 'the Pearl of Africa' because it has so much natural beauty, like big lakes and amazing animals!

Learning from This

Even though this was a very sad story, we can learn important lessons from it. We learn that actions have big consequences, and it's always best to be kind and follow rules. We also learn that there are brave grown-ups who work hard every day to keep us safe and make sure justice is done. And most importantly, we learn to be kind to each other and always tell a trusted grown-up if we are worried about anything. Remember, you are safe, and there are many people who care about you very much! Keep learning, keep playing, and keep being the amazing kids you are! Goodbye for now from your African news friend!
summary: In Uganda, a man who hurt little children at a nursery school was given the most serious punishment by a judge because of his very bad actions.
whyMatters: This story shows us that grown-ups like police and judges work hard to keep everyone, especially children, safe and ensure that people who do bad things face serious consequences. It also reminds us that our schools are safe places where kind adults look after us and that we should always tell a trusted grown-up if we feel worried.

title: Iran war live: Trump says no ‘early’ end to war, unhappy with Tehran offer

originalTitle: US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
title: US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
content: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

originalTitle: Trump considering option to ‘blast the hell out of’ Iran
title: Big Leaders Talk: Finding Ways to Be Friends!
content:
Jambo, my little explorers! It's your friendly reporter, Zola, here from our beautiful continent of Africa! I hope you're all having a wonderful day filled with sunshine and laughter. Today, we're going to talk about something important that big leaders in different countries do: they talk to each other to make sure everyone is safe and happy.

Hello from Far Away Lands!

Sometimes, countries are like very big families. Each family has a grown-up leader, maybe a mom, a dad, or a grandpa, who helps everyone get along. In the world, we have many, many countries, and each country also has a special leader, like a President or a Prime Minister. These leaders have a big job! Their job is to look after all the people in their country. They want to make sure everyone has food, goes to school, and lives in peace. Peace means no fighting, just everyone being safe and happy together. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent! It has 54 different countries, and each one is special and beautiful!

Meeting President Trump

Today, we heard a little bit about a leader from a country far, far away called the United States of America. His name is President Trump. He is a very important person in his country, and he works hard for his people. Imagine you have a friend who lives very far away. You might send them letters or talk on the phone. That's a bit like how leaders talk to each other, even if they live across oceans! Did you know? The United States of America is a very big country, with many different states, almost like provinces in our countries!

Learning About Iran

Now, there is another country called Iran. It's also a big country with its own special leaders and many wonderful people, just like us. Countries like Iran and the United States sometimes talk to each other about how to live peacefully in the world. Just like you might have friends from different schools or different towns, countries are friends with other countries too. They all share the same big world, so it's good for everyone to get along. Did you know? Iran is an ancient country with a very long history, full of amazing stories and beautiful art!

What is a "Peace Plan"?

Sometimes, leaders think about ways to make sure everyone stays friends and doesn't have arguments. They make what we call a 'peace plan.' A peace plan is like a big set of rules that everyone agrees to follow, so that there is no fighting and everyone can be safe. Think about when you play a game with your friends. You might make rules together, right? Rules like 'no pushing' or 'everyone gets a turn.' These rules help make sure the game is fun and fair for everyone. A peace plan is like those game rules, but for whole countries! Did you know? The United Nations is a big group where almost all the countries in the world come together to talk about peace and help each other!

When Plans Don't Make Everyone Happy

Our news today said that President Trump looked at a peace plan from Iran. But he said he was 'not happy' with it. Does that mean he was angry? Not exactly! It means he didn't think the plan was perfect yet. It's like when you and your friend are planning a game. Maybe your friend suggests playing hide-and-seek, but you really want to play soccer. You might say, 'I'm not happy with that plan,' not because you're angry, but because you have a different idea or you think it could be better. When President Trump said he was 'not happy,' it simply means that the leaders need to talk more. They need to find a way to make a new plan that makes everyone feel safe and understood. Did you know? Talking is one of the most powerful ways to solve problems! When we talk and listen, we can understand each other better.

How Leaders Find Solutions

So, what happens next? Do they shout? Do they fight? No, not at all! Good leaders always try to find solutions by talking and listening. This is very important. Imagine again with your friends. If you don't agree on a game, you don't start pushing! You sit down and talk. You say, 'What if we play soccer for a bit, and then hide-and-seek?' Or you try to find a totally new game that everyone likes. That's what leaders do! They keep talking. They send messages, they have meetings, and they try very hard to understand each other's ideas. They want to find a solution that works for everyone, so that all the people in their countries can live in peace. Did you know? In many African cultures, storytelling and talking together are very important ways to solve problems and keep communities strong!

Why Peace is Important for Everyone

Having peace in the world is super important for all of us, no matter where we live. When countries are peaceful, children can go to school, families can grow food, and everyone can feel safe and happy. It helps our continent of Africa too, because when the world is peaceful, we can all trade and learn from each other. It's like when your home is peaceful and calm. Everyone can play and learn and laugh. If there's lots of shouting, it's not very nice, is it? So, leaders work hard to make the whole world feel like a calm and happy home. We hope that President Trump and the leaders of Iran will keep talking and talking. We hope they find a wonderful peace plan that makes everyone happy and keeps the world a safe and friendly place for all children, everywhere. Did you know? Many wise people around the world have won special awards for working hard to bring peace to different places!

Our Hopes for Tomorrow

So, my dear friends, remember this: even when big people don't agree at first, talking and listening is always the best way. It shows kindness and understanding. It's how we build a better world for all of us. Let's always remember to be kind to our friends, listen to each other's ideas, and always try to find peaceful ways to solve any small disagreements we might have. Because when we learn to do it, big leaders learn too! Keep shining brightly, my little stars of Africa! Until next time, stay curious and keep spreading kindness!
summary: Big leaders like President Trump and leaders from Iran are talking to each other to find a special plan that helps all countries live in peace and friendship.
whyMatters: It matters because when big leaders talk and agree, it helps keep everyone in the world safe and happy, just like when you and your friends agree on rules for a fun game.

originalTitle: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
title: Helping Our Farms Grow Strong!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! Reporter Pendo here, bringing you some news about our food. Do you love delicious meals like ugali, rice, or yummy fruits? Of course, you do! All that wonderful food starts with happy, healthy plants in our farms. Today, we're talking about something very important that helps our plants grow super big and strong!

What is Special Plant Food?

Imagine you are a tiny seed. To grow into a tall plant, you need food, just like you need food to grow big and strong. Plants get some food from the soil, but sometimes, the soil needs a little extra help. That's where 'fertiliser' comes in! It's like a special vitamin boost for plants. It helps them grow leafy green, make strong roots, and produce lots of tasty fruits and vegetables for us to eat. Think of it as magic food for our farms! ***Did you know?*** Different plants like different kinds of special plant food! Some love it to grow big leaves, and some need it to make sweet fruits.

Why Do Our Farms Need This Special Food?

Our farmers work very hard to grow food for all of us. They plant seeds and take care of the soil. But after many harvests, the soil can get tired. It might not have enough 'food' left for new plants. When farmers use fertiliser, it makes the soil happy and full of good things again. This means more food can grow on the same farm! It helps grow more maize, millet, beans, and other important foods we eat every day.

Where Does Special Plant Food Come From?

This special plant food isn't just picked from a tree. It's made by big companies! They use special ingredients from different places around the world. These ingredients are mixed together carefully to make the best food for our plants. Some of these special ingredients have to travel a very, very long way to get to the factories. Then, the special plant food travels to our farms to help our food grow. It's like a big journey! ***Did you know?*** Some of the important ingredients for plant food come from deep underground, like special rocks and minerals!

Trouble in a Faraway Place

Now, here's where we need to think about a problem. Sometimes, there is trouble in a faraway land. Maybe people are not getting along, or there are difficulties that make it hard for things to move around. When there are problems like this, it can be tough to get the special ingredients needed to make plant food. Imagine if the truck that brings your favourite snacks couldn't drive because of a big storm far away. It's a bit like that! A very important person, who runs a big company called Yara that makes plant food, said this is happening. He worries it's getting harder to make enough plant food for everyone.

What Happens When There's Not Enough Plant Food?

If our farmers can't get enough of this special plant food, their plants might not grow as big and healthy. This means they will grow less food. Imagine fewer sweet mangoes or fewer crunchy carrots! When plants don't grow much food, there won't be enough for everyone. This is called a 'shortage'. It means there are not enough yummy fruits, vegetables, and grains to go around for all the families. ***Did you know?*** Maize (corn) is a super important food for many families in Africa! If maize plants don't get enough food, we'll have less ugali or porridge.

Food Might Cost More Money

When there is less food available, what happens? Think about it: if there are only a few toys left in the shop that everyone wants, they might become more expensive. It's the same with food. If there isn't much food, then the food that *is* available might become more expensive for families to buy. This makes it harder for mums and dads to buy enough food for everyone at home. No one wants food to cost too much, because everyone deserves to eat well every day! We all need good, healthy food to play, learn, and grow.

What Can We Do to Help?

It's important for people all over the world to work together to solve problems. We want to make sure farmers can always get what they need to grow food. We can also help by not wasting food. Every little bit counts! Eating all your vegetables and fruits helps make sure food is used well. Some communities are also learning to grow food in clever new ways, even without lots of special plant food. Sharing knowledge and helping neighbours can make a big difference! ***Did you know?*** Growing a small garden at home, even in a pot, can help! You can grow your own herbs or tomatoes.

Let's Keep Our Farms Happy!

So, you see, even a small problem far away can affect the food we eat here in Africa. But don't worry! People are thinking about this and working hard to find solutions. By understanding how our food grows and what helps our farmers, we can all appreciate our meals even more. Let's hope for happy plants and lots of yummy food for everyone!
summary: A big company says that problems in a faraway land might make it hard to get special plant food, which could mean less food for everyone and higher prices for yummy meals.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone needs good, healthy food to grow strong, play, and learn. If food becomes harder to get or costs too much, families might struggle to eat well.

content: No club has ever been relegated with 43 points at the end of a Premier League season - so have Leeds already done enough to secure survival?

title: Leeds beat Burnley to move closer to safety
